title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Illinois
description: Illinois is holding a regular U.S. Senate election for the Class II seat currently held by Dick Durbin, who announced he would not seek re-election. The race matters because it is an open seat in a large, reliably Democratic state, making the Democratic primary the decisive contest and giving the winner a strong advantage in the general election. The campaign has centered on experience, party leadership, and how aggressively to confront the Trump administration, with candidates also emphasizing affordability, health care, and labor issues. The general election is expected to reflect Illinois’s partisan lean, while the primary highlighted different styles of Democratic leadership and coalition-building.

candidates.name: Juliana Stratton
candidates.summary: Juliana Stratton is the lieutenant governor of Illinois and a former member of the Illinois House of Representatives. She ran for the open U.S. Senate seat in 2026 and won the Democratic nomination, positioning herself as a continuation of the Pritzker administration’s agenda and a progressive voice on issues such as health care and economic fairness.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Illinois, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Lieutenant Governor
career_history.description: Serves as Illinois’s 48th lieutenant governor, elected on the Pritzker ticket.

career_history.title: Member
career_history.description: Represented the 5th district in the Illinois House.

career_history.title: Executive Director
career_history.description: Led county criminal justice reform coordination efforts.

career_history.title: Director
career_history.organization: Center for Public Safety and Justice, University of Illinois at Chicago
career_history.description: Directed a public safety and justice research center.

candidates.name: Raja Krishnamoorthi
candidates.summary: Raja Krishnamoorthi is a U.S. representative from Illinois’s 8th congressional district and a former state and federal policy aide. He ran for the Senate in 2026, emphasizing his congressional experience, anti-corruption and oversight work, and a message focused on working families, affordability, and opposition to Donald Trump’s policies.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Illinois, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: Special Assistant Attorney General
career_history.description: Helped start the state’s anti-corruption unit under the Illinois attorney general.

career_history.title: President
career_history.organization: High-tech small businesses in the Chicago area
career_history.description: Led small businesses before entering Congress.

candidates.name: Robin Kelly
candidates.summary: Robin Kelly is a U.S. representative from Illinois’s 2nd congressional district and previously served in the Illinois House and in state and county government. She ran for the Senate in 2026, presenting herself as an experienced legislator focused on health care, gun violence prevention, and economic issues, with an emphasis on representing diverse urban, suburban, and rural constituencies.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Illinois, 2026 - Ballotpedia
